sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method with dual implementations: equalized method (f=0.09, recalculates f$ after each trade) vs. nonequalized method (f=0.17, fixed f$ per trade). Position size = (optimal_f × cumulative_equity) / underlying_price. Equalized method recommended for dynamic account growth.

source_rule_text: Position size is determined by dividing the f$ value (optimal f multiplied by account equity) by the underlying price, with two distinct methods: equalized method recalculates f$ after each trade, while nonequalized method uses a fixed f$ value.
